---
title: "Session 3 · Dummies & the *t*-Test"
---

## Overview

What happens when the predictor is a category rather than a number? In this session we plug a single 0/1 dummy variable into the regression we just learned and discover that it encodes a difference between two group means. Remarkably, this regression reproduces the classic two-sample *t*-test exactly --- our first concrete demonstration of the course's "specific → general" arc, in which a familiar test turns out to be a special case of regression.

## Learning objectives

By the end of this session, you will be able to:

- Code a categorical variable as a 0/1 dummy predictor.
- Interpret the dummy coefficient as a difference between two group means.
- Show that a regression on a single dummy reproduces the two-sample *t*-test.
- Read the intercept as the mean of the reference group.
- Connect the *t*-statistic and *p*-value from `lm()` to those from `t.test()`.

## This session's materials

- {{< fa book-open-reader >}} **Read:** [Chapter 1: Simple Linear Regression](/notes/chapters/01-simple-linear-regression.html){target="_blank"}
- {{< fa laptop-code >}} **Lab:** [Lab 2: Simple Linear Regression](/labs/labs/lab02-simple-regression.html){target="_blank"}
- {{< fa pencil >}} **Homework due:** [Homework 1: Simple Linear Regression](/assignment/hw01.html)
- {{< fa circle-question >}} **Practice:** [Practice Quiz 1](/practice/chapters/quiz1-simple-regression.html){target="_blank"}
- {{< fa circle-check >}} **Graded quiz:** [Module Quiz 1](/assignment/quizzes.html#quiz-1)

## Key concepts

| Concept | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| **Dummy variable** | A 0/1 indicator that encodes membership in a category. |
| **Reference group** | The category coded 0, against which the other group is compared. |
| **Group mean difference** | The gap between the two groups' average outcomes, captured by the dummy coefficient. |
| **Two-sample *t*-test** | A test comparing the means of two independent groups. |
| **Dummy coefficient** | The estimated difference in means between the coded group and the reference group. |
| **Regression–*t*-test equivalence** | The fact that a regression on one dummy yields the same inference as the two-sample *t*-test. |
| **Intercept (with a dummy)** | The mean outcome of the reference group. |
